verbatim_quote: "He had three aboard the Silence, Quellon Humble had confided in a whisper. Queer men and terrible, they were, but the Crow's Eye had made them slaves."
note: Magical trappings: three warlocks/wizards enslaved on Euron's ship Silence

verbatim_quote: "And then he saw her: a single-masted galley, lean and low, with a dark red hull. Her sails, now furled, were black as a starless sky. Even at anchor Silence looked both cruel and fast. On her prow was a black iron maiden with one arm outstretched. Her waist was slender, her breasts high and proud, her legs long and shapely. A windblown mane of black iron hair streamed from her head, and her eyes were mother-of-pearl, but she had no mouth."
"His Grace was crossing a bridge at Pyke when he fell and was dashed upon the rocks below."
note: Balon's death from falling off bridge; context confirms sudden death enabling Euron's return and claim to throne

verbatim_quote: "Euron Greyjoy climbed the hill slowly, with every eye upon him."
note: Euron addresses kingsmoot via his campaign; fuller context: he won via combination of gifts, horn display, and grandiose promises

verbatim_quote: "And so shall we," Euron Greyjoy promised. "That horn you heard I found amongst the smoking ruins that were Valyria, where no man has dared to walk but me. You heard its call, and felt its power. It is a dragon horn, bound with bands of red gold and Valyrian steel graven with enchantments. The dragonlords of old sounded such horns, before the Doom devoured them. With this horn, ironmen, I can bind dragons to my will."
note: Dragonbinder horn shown at kingsmoot; Euron explicitly claims dragon-binding power and intent to sail to Daenerys

verbatim_quote: "Now he has given them conquest, and they are his for good and all, the captain thought. The taste was bitter on his tongue. This was my victory, not his. Where was he? Back on Oakenshield, lazing in a castle. He stole my wife and he stole my throne, and now he steals my glory."
note: Victarion resentful of Euron; broader context: Euron seduced Victarion's wife, leading to her death; now sends him east with Dragonbinder on behalf of Euron's dragon ambitions
Dragonbinder 'true master' concept not found in searched passages; horn shown but horn-binding mechanics not detailed

verbatim_quote: "Bran was falling, falling, falling off the castle, and when he woke in his bed he found he could not move his legs anymore."
note: Parallel established: Bran tower fall parallels Euron's claimed flying dream; Bran visited by three-eyed crow (Bloodraven); the text implies maester was wrong about physical limitation

verbatim_quote: "Balon has fallen, Balon my brother, who honored the Old Way and paid the iron price. Balon the Brave, Balon the Blessed, Balon Twice-Crowned, who won us back our freedoms and our god."
note: Euron called 'Crow's Eye' with one red eye; Bloodraven is 'three-eyed crow' with one red eye; parallel naming and symbolic connection established

verbatim_quote: "Godless? Why, Aeron, I am the godliest man ever to raise sail! You serve one god, Damphair, but I have served ten thousand."
note: Euron claims power over men's minds and worship; while not explicitly 'dream-walking', Euron's control over Aeron later established via visions/dreams in ADWD

verbatim_quote: "And suddenly Bran was soaring. The world spread below him like a map."
note: Bran's dream shows 'the whole world' from above including Asshai and dragons; video speculation that Euron saw same in childhood dream

verbatim_quote: "IRONMEN," said Euron Greyjoy, "you have heard my horn. Now hear my words. I am Balon's brother, Quellon's eldest living son. Lord Vickon's blood is in my veins, and the blood of the Old Kraken. Yet I have sailed farther than any of them. Only one living kraken has never known defeat. Only one has never bent his knee. Only one has sailed to Asshai by the Shadow, and seen wonders and terrors beyond imagining . . ."
note: Euron claims voyage to Asshai; establishes Asshai as locus of power and sorcery

verbatim_quote: "That horn you heard I found amongst the smoking ruins that were Valyria, where no man has dared to walk but me."
note: Euron claims direct voyage to Valyria ruins; possession of Valyrian steel items (sword, horn bands) supports the claim

verbatim_quote: "Shade-of-the-evening, the wine of the warlocks. I came upon a cask of it when I captured a certain galleas out of Qarth, along with some cloves and nutmeg, forty bolts of green silk, and four warlocks who told a curious tale. One presumed to threaten me, so I killed him and fed him to the other three."
note: Euron claims to have captured warlocks and their psychedelic drug; the warlocks stained his lips blue/bruised (described throughout text)
